mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding labor demand and income growth, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. This relationship is persistent across business cycles.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return rank correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month change is less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it signals a softening labor market that typically precedes economic contraction. This leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as earnings expectations downgrade.
failure_condition: If the median 60-day forward return following UNRATE > 4.5% is positive, or if the trigger occurs fewer than 3 times over the backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in industrial production and elevated federal funds rate signal a monetary tightening that is not matched by economic expansion, compressing profit margins and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The negative effect is amplified when both conditions hold.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month negative for 3 straight months and FEDFUNDS > 4.0
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return when both conditions are true is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or if the interaction term in a linear model is not significant.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime where the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, further increases or sustained high levels restrict credit growth and raise discount rates, depressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. The effect is regime-dependent; in low-rate regimes, rate increases may be positive.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return conditional on FEDFUNDS > 4% is not significantly less than the unconditional median (Mann-Whitney p > 0.10), or if the direction flips to positive in this regime during out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1,400 thousand units signals weakening residential investment, a leading indicator of economic slowdown. This contraction in demand reduces corporate earnings prospects, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return following HOUST < 1400 is positive or if fewer than 5 trigger events occur in the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales reflect strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-trading-day horizon as market participants price in better consumption data.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return rank correlation with RSAFS month-over-month change is less than 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining real GDP growth and elevated consumer price inflation indicates stagflation, a regime that severely hurts equity valuations through falling profits and rising discount rates. The negative effect on 60-day forward returns is pronounced when both conditions are present simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 0% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 3%
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return in stagflation quarters is not negative (t-test p > 0.10) or if the interaction term in a linear regression of returns on growth and inflation has a p-value > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 100 index points, it signals a contraction in the manufacturing sector that precedes broader economic weakness. This negative supply shock reduces corporate earnings and leads to lower S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the median 20-day forward return after INDPRO < 100 is positive, or if the signal occurs with less than 10% frequency in the sample.
